caption:
Here are the best eats that I had on my trip to JAPAN!

10. Godaime Hanayama Udon (Tokyo)
9. MookEN (Osaka)
8. T Nakameguro (Tokyo)
7. Harajuku Gyoza Ru (Tokyo)
6. Hikiniku To Come (Kyoto)

My Top 5 eats from Japan video drops tomorrow so you won’t want to miss it!
transcript: Here are the top 10 things I ate on my 3-week trip across Japan as someone who planned his entire trip around trying the best foods. I'm going to let you know where to go and what to get. Number 10 is the udon set at Godaime Hanayama in Tokyo. These extra wide udon noodles are served cold and you dip them right into the sauces. They're so incredibly chewy, I've never had any texture like them. They also come with some delightful tempura as well. And pro tip, the line gets crazy here so get there early. And second pro tip, they actually sell the udon right in their store, so stock up after you eat inside. Number 9 might have the longest line out of any spot on the list. It's all for these little crispy cream puff donuts. The spot is called Muken in Osaka. These aren't your average cream puffs. They're delightfully light, crispy, and almost caramelly. One bite in and I know exactly why they're so famous among Osaka locals and why they sell it every day. Number 8 is the Wagyu Omakase Experience at T Nakameguro in Tokyo. They're cutting a fat T-bone Wagyu steak and grilling them up as part of the omakase. Overall, a really fun and delicious experience. Number 7 is the best gyoza I had on my trip and the best I've ever had. Harajuku gyoza roux in Tokyo. Now you can get them either boiled or pan fried. They're cheap and go down so easily. This is actually one of Chrissy's favorites from the trip. Number 6 is a hamburger, not even a cheeseburger. It's in Kyoto at a spot called Hikiniki to come. They only do one thing, burgers with rice. Their burgers are first grilled, then open roasted, and you eat them with rice, no buns here. The amount of flavor and moisture in these burgers is unlike anything I've ever had. One of my favorite dinners on the trip. In tomorrow's video, I'm going to share my top 5 eats which include a Michelin-rated ramen, cheap incredible soft serve, and more. I promise you won't want to miss it. So as always, follow up for more food adventures.
